reasoning: Early in March 2025, during his inauguration, Uruguayan President Yamandú Orsi received a direct, public invitation from Brazil's President Lula da Silva to attend the BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro in July 2025. Shortly after, the Uruguayan newspaper El País published an article citing high-level government sources confirming that Orsi "will attend as a guest of his Brazilian colleague." Furthermore, around mid-March 2025, Uruguay's Foreign Minister Mario Lubetkin publicly referenced the government's plans to take a more active role in the BRICS bloc, starting with accepting the invitation to the July summit. Given these very explicit confirmations from government figures to top-tier national media and directly via ministerial speeches, the resolution criteria (a public communication written or oral by the government or its representative confirming acceptance of the invitation to attend the summit) is effectively met, or rests on such a solid foundation that it is almost certain to be met before the July 1, 2025 deadline. There's virtually no indicator that Orsi would reverse course following these statements.
